hives Ed Morrissey Show Newsletters Social Security deficit slides to worst showing in a generation posted at 3:35 pm on January 7, 2010 by Ed Morrissey Share on Facebook 1 1 SHARES Steve at No Runny Eggs has kept a close eye on the financial statements from the Social Security administration, and he has good reason to do so. Earlier this year, we began to see monthly deficits from SSA, a result of income drops as the recession and unemployment deepened. However, with the Obama administration arguing that the economy has already begun its recovery, the deficits at SSA haven’t. Social Security has a monthly income of a little more that a quarter of a trillion, and a monthly outgo of a little more that a quarter of a trillion. However, the outgo has outstripped the income, and the monthly deficits at SSA have more that doubled. Social Security has a monthly income of a little more that a quarter

I expect is that when I click the button, the transitions starts and ends 5 seconds later. What happens is that after I clicked the button, it delays for about 3,5 seconds before the transition starts and the transition lasts for about 1,5 seconds. So all in all, 5 seconds. If I use transition-: Q: Why is my CSS3 transition delaying before it starts? Either I've misunderstood how it's working, or it simply isn't working. I have the following CSS: transition-property: max-width; transition-timing-function: linear; transition-duration: 5s; transition-delay: 0s; What I expect is that when I click the button, the transitions starts and ends 5 seconds later. However, what happens in reality may depend on how exactly you are applying this CSS transition. One likely reason for your experience could be due to an animation or layout property which delays before changing its state. In modern browsers such as Chrome version 65+ , Firefox 70+, Safari 13+ and Edge 17+, a new property has been introduced named `offset-duration`. This causes animations (which are transitions applied via the "animation" shorthand) to offset their timing function by a specified duration, giving the illusion of delays. All patients underwent ERNA within 5 days post-pacemaker implantation and follow-up studies at 6 and 12 months. Standard deviation of LV mean phase angle (SD LV mPA) exPressed in degrees, which was derived by Fourier first harmonic analysis of phase images, was used to quantify left intraventricular dyssynchrony.
